title: A precision instrument dial showing exactly five valid state positions with a needle pointing at one. The valid states are the only marked positions on the dial; the needle always points to exactly one — the current state. This is Precept's determinism made visual: the same definition and data always produce the same reading, and the only readings that exist are the ones declared in the contract.
conceptualBridge: The precision dial means Precept's definition declares the only valid states because a calibrated instrument only has marked positions at values that exist — the needle can only point at declared states, and Precept structurally prevents any other reading.
decision: Rejected: 3.81 avg below threshold — the dial's thin lines and many elements fail at small scale; legibility at 32px is 3 (minimum) and visual economy score of 3 indicates over-complexity. The 'precision instrument' concept is worth revisiting with a much simpler form: just the ring + 3 bold tick marks + needle, no subdivisions.
